title: 50/30/20 Family Budget Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Master the 50/30/20 budget rule. Calculate needs, wants & savings for your household—free tool.
intro: Personal and household finances follow universal principles: [compound interest](/calculadora-interes-compuesto-aporte-mensual-crecimiento), diversification, long-term vision, and low costs. Tools that work in any market worldwide. Allocate your family budget across needs, wants, and savings. Data is current for 2026 and reviewed periodically to keep you informed.

useCases: Quick reference for personal finance projects

explanation:
**Personal and household finance** use universal mathematical principles (compound interest, time value of money, risk-return optimization). This calculator applies industry-standard formulas.

## How it's calculated

Family needs, wants, and savings allocation.

This calculator applies the formula with the values you enter, checking typical ranges and alerting when values seem unusual.

## Typical benchmarks by time horizon

| Time Horizon | S&P 500 | 10y Bonds | Cash |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 year | Highly variable | 4-5% | 3-5% |
| 5 years | ~8% avg | ~4% | [Inflation rate](/inflacion-argentina) |
| 10 years | ~9-10% | ~4% | Below inflation |
| 20+ years | ~10% nominal | ~5% | N/A |

## Investment principles

- **Stick to the plan** during downturns. Emotion is the worst advisor.
- **Diversify**: don't concentrate in one asset, sector, or country.
- **Keep costs low**: Expense ratios >1% annually reduce 30% of your returns over 30 years.
- **Long time horizon**: Stocks work over 10+ years, not in 1 year.
- **Rebalance**: Once per year, no more.

## Final notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. Data is current for 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: How does the 50/30/20 budget rule work?
faq.a: Allocate 50% to needs (housing, food, utilities), 30% to wants (entertainment, dining out), and 20% to savings and debt repayment.
A simple framework to allocate your income across three categories: essentials (50%), discretionary spending (30%), and financial goals (20%).

faq.q: How do I use this calculator?
faq.a: Enter your monthly or annual income, and the tool calculates the recommended amounts for needs, wants, and savings based on the 50/30/20 rule.

faq.q: What counts as needs versus wants?
faq.a: Needs include housing, utilities, groceries, and essential transportation. Wants include entertainment, dining out, hobbies, and non-essential purchases.
While 50/30/20 is the recommended baseline, you can adjust percentages based on your situation—for example, 60/20/20 if you have higher housing costs.
Yes, it's a proven framework recommended by financial experts. It's simple to understand and flexible enough to adapt to most household situations.

faq.q: Is my data private and secure?
faq.a: Yes. All calculations happen in your browser. We don't send any of your data to external servers.
Results are directional and within ±5% accuracy. The 50/30/20 rule is a guideline—adjust based on your actual financial situation and goals.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, does not require external data updates
